    13→/**
    14→ * VulnerabilityExceptionRequest entity representing a request to create an exception for a vulnerability.
    15→ *
    16→ * Workflow:
    17→ * - Regular users create requests with PENDING status (requires approval)
    18→ * - ADMIN/SECCHAMPION users get auto-approved requests (status immediately APPROVED)
    19→ * - ADMIN/SECCHAMPION can approve or reject PENDING requests
    20→ * - Approved requests create corresponding VulnerabilityException entries
    21→ *
    22→ * State machine:
    23→ * - PENDING → APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED
    24→ * - APPROVED → EXPIRED
    25→ * - REJECTED, CANCELLED, EXPIRED → (terminal states)
    26→ *
    27→ * Related to: Feature 031-vuln-exception-approval (Vulnerability Exception Request & Approval Workflow)
    28→ *
    29→ * @property id Unique identifier
    30→ * @property vulnerability Vulnerability this request applies to
    31→ * @property requestedByUser User who submitted the request (nullable for audit after user deletion)
    32→ * @property requestedByUsername Username of requester (preserved for audit trail)
    33→ * @property scope Exception scope (SINGLE_VULNERABILITY or CVE_PATTERN)
    34→ * @property reason Business justification (50-2048 characters)
    35→ * @property expirationDate When the exception should expire
    36→ * @property status Current status (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, EXPIRED, CANCELLED)
    37→ * @property autoApproved True if ADMIN/SECCHAMPION auto-approved
    38→ * @property reviewedByUser Reviewer who approved/rejected (nullable for audit after user deletion)
    39→ * @property reviewedByUsername Username of reviewer (preserved for audit trail)
    40→ * @property reviewDate When the request was reviewed
    41→ * @property reviewComment Reviewer's notes/justification
    42→ * @property createdAt When the request was created
    43→ * @property updatedAt When the request was last modified
    44→ * @property version Optimistic locking version for concurrency control
    45→ */
    46→@Entity
    47→@Table(
    48→    name = "vulnerability_exception_request",
    49→    indexes = [
    50→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_req_vulnerability", columnList = "vulnerability_id"),
    51→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_req_status", columnList = "status"),
    52→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_req_requester", columnList = "requested_by_user_id"),
    53→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_req_reviewer", columnList = "reviewed_by_user_id"),
    54→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_req_created", columnList = "created_at"),
    55→        Index(name = "idx_vuln_req_expiration", columnList = "expiration_date")
    56→    ]
    57→)
    58→@Serdeable
    59→data class VulnerabilityExceptionRequest(
    60→    @Id
    61→    @GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.IDENTITY)
    62→    var id: Long? = null,
    63→
    64→    /**
    65→     * Vulnerability this request applies to
    66→     * ManyToOne relationship with nullable for orphaned requests (vulnerability deleted)
    67→     *
    68→     * FK constraint: ON DELETE SET NULL - Preserves exception requests for audit trail
    69→     * when vulnerability is deleted (e.g., via asset cascade deletion)
    70→     */
    71→    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
    72→    @JoinColumn(name = "vulnerability_id", nullable = true)
    73→    @OnDelete(action = OnDeleteAction.SET_NULL)
    74→    @JsonIgnore // Prevent lazy loading errors during JSON serialization
    75→    var vulnerability: Vulnerability? = null,
    76→
    77→    /**
    78→     * User who requested the exception
    79→     * Nullable: If user deleted, preserve username for audit trail
    80→     */
    81→    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
    82→    @JoinColumn(name = "requested_by_user_id")
    83→    @JsonIgnore
    84→    var requestedByUser: User? = null,
    85→
    86→    /**
    87→     * Username of requester (denormalized for audit trail)
    88→     * Preserved even if user account deleted
    89→     */
    90→    @Column(name = "requested_by_username", nullable = false, length = 255)
    91→    @NotBlank
    92→    @Size(max = 255)
    93→    var requestedByUsername: String,
    94→
    95→    /**
    96→     * Exception scope: SINGLE_VULNERABILITY or CVE_PATTERN
    97→     */
    98→    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)
    99→    @Column(name = "scope", nullable = false, length = 20)
   100→    @NotNull
   101→    var scope: ExceptionScope,
   102→
   103→    /**
   104→     * Business justification for the exception
   105→     * Required: 50-2048 characters, plain text only (XSS sanitized)
   106→     */
   107→    @Column(name = "reason", nullable = false, length = 2048)
   108→    @NotBlank
   109→    @Size(min = 50, max = 2048)
   110→    var reason: String,
   111→
   112→    /**
   113→     * When the exception should expire

   121→     * Current status: PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, EXPIRED, CANCELLED

   126→    var status: ExceptionRequestStatus,

   138→    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
   139→    @JoinColumn(name = "reviewed_by_user_id")
   140→    @JsonIgnore
   141→    var reviewedByUser: User? = null,

   211→
   212→            if (!isOwner && !isAdminOrSecChampion) {

   223→            HttpResponse.ok(request)

   234→    /**
   235→     * TASK-020: Approve a pending exception request.
   236→     *
   237→     * POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}/approve
   238→     * Auth: ADMIN or SECCHAMPION only
   239→     * Concurrency: First-approver-wins (optimistic locking)
   240→     *
   241→     * @param id Request ID
   242→     * @param reviewDto Optional review comment
   243→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user (reviewer)
   244→     * @param request HTTP request for IP address extraction
   245→     * @return 200 OK with updated request, 404 if not found, 409 if concurrent approval
   246→     */
   247→    @Post("/{id}/approve")
   248→    @Secured("ADMIN", "SECCHAMPION")
   249→    open fun approveRequest(
   250→        @PathVariable id: Long,
   251→        @Nullable @Body reviewDto: ReviewExceptionRequestDto?,
   252→        authentication: Authentication,
   253→        request: HttpRequest<*>
   254→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   255→        return try {
   256→            val reviewerId = getUserId(authentication)
   257→            val clientIp = request.remoteAddress.address.hostAddress
   258→
   259→            logger.debug("Approving request: requestId={}, reviewerId={}", id, reviewerId)
   260→
   261→            val approvedRequest = exceptionRequestService.approveRequest(
   262→                requestId = id,
   263→                reviewerUserId = reviewerId,
   264→                reviewDto = reviewDto,
   265→                clientIp = clientIp
   266→            )
   267→
   268→            logger.info("Request approved: requestId={}, reviewerId={}", id, reviewerId)
   269→
   270→            HttpResponse.ok(approvedRequest)
   271→
   272→        } catch (e: ConcurrentApprovalException) {
   273→            // TASK-025: Handle concurrent approval conflicts
   274→            logger.warn("Concurrent approval conflict: requestId={}, {}", id, e.getDetailedMessage())
   275→            HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.CONFLICT).body(
   276→                ErrorResponse(
   277→                    error = e.getUserMessage(),
   278→                    details = e.getDetailedMessage(),
   279→                    status = 409
   280→                )
   281→            )
   282→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   283→            logger.warn("Invalid approval request: requestId={}, error={}", id, e.message)
   284→            HttpResponse.notFound<VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto>()
   285→        } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
   286→            // TASK-026: Handle invalid status transitions
   287→            logger.warn("Invalid status transition: requestId={}, error={}", id, e.message)
   288→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(
   289→                error = "Invalid status transition",
   290→                details = e.message,
   291→                status = 400
   292→            ))
   293→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   294→            logger.error("Failed to approve request: requestId={}", id, e)
   295→            HttpResponse.serverError<VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto>()
   296→        }
   297→    }

   502→    /**
   503→     * GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/statistics
   504→     *
   505→     * Get statistical metrics for exception requests.
   506→     *
   507→     * **Access Control**: ADMIN and SECCHAMPION roles only
   508→     *
   509→     * **Query Parameters**:
   510→     * - dateRange: Optional (7days, 30days, 90days, alltime), default 30days
   511→     *
   512→     * **Response**: 200 OK with ExceptionStatisticsDto containing:
   513→     * - totalRequests: Total count in date range
   514→     * - approvalRatePercent: Approval percentage (null if no reviewed requests)
   515→     * - averageApprovalTimeHours: Median approval time in hours (null if no approved requests)
   516→     * - requestsByStatus: Map of status → count
   517→     * - topRequesters: Top 10 requesters with counts
   518→     * - topCVEs: Top 10 CVEs with counts
   519→     *
   520→     * Feature: 031-vuln-exception-approval
   521→     * User Story 8: Analytics & Reporting (P3)
   522→     * Phase 11: Analytics & Reporting
   523→     * Reference: spec.md acceptance scenario US8-1
   524→     */
   525→    @Get("/statistics")
   526→    @Secured("ADMIN", "SECCHAMPION")
   527→    open fun getStatistics(
   528→        authentication: Authentication,
   529→        @QueryValue @Nullable dateRange: String?
   530→    ): HttpResponse<ExceptionStatisticsDto> {
   531→        return try {
   532→            val effectiveDateRange = dateRange ?: "30days"
   533→
   534→            logger.info("Statistics requested: dateRange={}, user={}", effectiveDateRange, authentication.name)
   535→
   536→            // Get metrics from statistics service
   537→            val approvalRate = statisticsService.getApprovalRate(effectiveDateRange)
   538→            val averageApprovalTime = statisticsService.getAverageApprovalTime(effectiveDateRange)
   539→            val requestsByStatus = statisticsService.getRequestsByStatus(effectiveDateRange)
   540→            val topRequesters = statisticsService.getTopRequesters(10, effectiveDateRange)
   541→            val topCVEs = statisticsService.getTopCVEs(10, effectiveDateRange)
   542→
   543→            // Calculate total requests
   544→            val totalRequests = requestsByStatus.values.sum()
   545→
   546→            // Convert status map to string keys for JSON serialization
   547→            val statusMap = requestsByStatus.mapKeys { it.key.name }
   548→
   549→            // Convert to DTOs
   550→            val topRequesterDtos = topRequesters.map { (username, count) ->
   551→                TopRequesterDto(username, count)
   552→            }
   553→            val topCVEDtos = topCVEs.map { (cveId, count) ->
   554→                TopCVEDto(cveId, count)
   555→            }
   556→
   557→            val statistics = ExceptionStatisticsDto(
   558→                totalRequests = totalRequests,
   559→                approvalRatePercent = approvalRate,
   560→                averageApprovalTimeHours = averageApprovalTime,

- **Path**: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/domain/VulnerabilityExceptionRequest.kt`
- **Relationship**: ManyToOne with Vulnerability (line 71-75, nullable with ON DELETE SET NULL)
- **State Machine**: PENDING → APPROVED/REJECTED/CANCELLED → terminal states
- **Audit Fields**: Denormalized usernames (`requestedByUsername`, `reviewedByUsername`) preserved for audit trail
- **Concurrency Control**: Optimistic locking with `@Version` field

**3. VulnerabilityExceptionRequestController**
- **Path**: `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/controller/VulnerabilityExceptionRequestController.kt`
- **Key Endpoints**:
  - `POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests` - Create request (auto-approval for ADMIN/SECCHAMPION)
  - `GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/my?status=&page=&size=` - User's own requests
  - `GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/my/summary` - Summary statistics
  - `GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}` - Get request by ID
  - `POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}/approve` - Approve (ADMIN/SECCHAMPION)
  - `POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}/reject` - Reject (ADMIN/SECCHAMPION)
  - `DELETE /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}` - Cancel own request
  - `GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/pending` - Get pending (ADMIN/SECCHAMPION)
  - `GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/statistics` - Analytics (ADMIN/SECCHAMPION)
  - `GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/export` - Excel export (ADMIN/SECCHAMPION)

### Key Observations

1. **CVE ID Field Naming Mismatch**: 
   - Backend DTO uses: `vulnerabilityCve: String?`
   - Frontend expects: `vulnerabilityCveId: string | null`
   - This is a **potential bug** - the field names don't match between backend and frontend

2. **Field Display Consistency**:
   - All components safely handle null CVE IDs with fallback text ("Unknown", "Remediated", or "-")
   - CveLink component validates CVE format before rendering as link

3. **Access Control**:
   - Users can only view/cancel their own requests (except ADMIN/SECCHAMPION who can view all)
   - Only ADMIN/SECCHAMPION can approve/reject requests
   - Auto-approval triggers immediately for ADMIN/SECCHAMPION requesters

4. **Feature Location**: Feature 031-vuln-exception-approval is referenced throughout

package com.secman.dto

import com.secman.domain.ExceptionRequestStatus
import com.secman.domain.ExceptionScope
import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable
import java.time.LocalDateTime

/**
 * DTO for vulnerability exception request responses.
 *
 * Used for GET endpoints returning exception request data:
 * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}
 * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/my
 * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/pending
 *
 * Contains all request details including embedded vulnerability information
 * for display without additional queries.
 *
 * Feature: 031-vuln-exception-approval
 * Reference: contracts/exception-request-api.yaml lines 575-625
 */
@Serdeable
data class VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto(
    /**
     * Unique identifier of the exception request
     */
    val id: Long,

    /**
     * ID of the vulnerability this request applies to
     */
    val vulnerabilityId: Long?,

    /**
     * CVE ID of the vulnerability (denormalized for display)
     */
    val vulnerabilityCve: String?,

    /**
     * Name of the asset with the vulnerability (denormalized for display)
     */
    val assetName: String?,

    /**
     * IP address of the asset (denormalized for display)
     */
    val assetIp: String?,

    /**
     * Username of the person who requested the exception
     */
    val requestedByUsername: String,

    /**
     * Exception scope: SINGLE_VULNERABILITY or CVE_PATTERN
     */
    val scope: ExceptionScope,

    /**
     * Business justification for the exception (50-2048 characters)
     */
    val reason: String,

    /**
     * When the exception should expire
     */
    val expirationDate: LocalDateTime,

    /**
     * Current status: PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, EXPIRED, CANCELLED
     */
    val status: ExceptionRequestStatus,

    /**
     * True if this request was auto-approved (requester has ADMIN/SECCHAMPION role)
     */
    val autoApproved: Boolean,

    /**
     * Username of the reviewer who approved/rejected
     * Null if not yet reviewed
     */
    val reviewedByUsername: String?,

    /**
     * When the request was reviewed (approved or rejected)
     * Null if not yet reviewed
     */
    val reviewDate: LocalDateTime?,

    /**
     * Reviewer's comment/justification
     * Optional for approval, required for rejection
     * Null if not reviewed or no comment provided
     */
    val reviewComment: String?,

    /**
     * When the request was created
     */
    val createdAt: LocalDateTime,

    /**
     * When the request was last updated
     */
    val updatedAt: LocalDateTime
)

There it is. Now I have the full picture. Here's the fix plan:

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
**Root Cause: JSON field name mismatch between backend and frontend**

When Micronaut serializes a Kotlin data class, the JSON field names match the Kotlin property names exactly. The backend DTO has `vulnerabilityCve`, but the frontend TypeScript interface expects `vulnerabilityCveId`. Since JSON deserialization in JavaScript is permissive (no runtime type checking), the field simply comes through as `undefined` — and the fallback `|| 'Unknown'` kicks in, showing the red "Unknown" text you see.

Key points:
1. **Backend** (`VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto.kt:37`): field is named `vulnerabilityCve`
2. **Frontend** (`exceptionRequestService.ts:38`): interface expects `vulnerabilityCveId`
3. The JSON response contains `{"vulnerabilityCve": "CVE-2024-XXXX", ...}` but the frontend reads `request.vulnerabilityCveId` which is `undefined`
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

## Fix Plan

**Option A (Recommended): Fix the frontend to match the backend**

Change the frontend TypeScript interface and all component references from `vulnerabilityCveId` to `vulnerabilityCve`. This is the simpler fix since the backend is already deployed and serving data with this field name.

Files to change:
1. **`src/frontend/src/services/exceptionRequestService.ts:38`** — rename `vulnerabilityCveId` → `vulnerabilityCve` in the interface
2. **`src/frontend/src/components/ExceptionRequestDetailModal.tsx:177`** — `request.vulnerabilityCveId` → `request.vulnerabilityCve`
3. **`src/frontend/src/components/MyExceptionRequests.tsx`** — any reference to `vulnerabilityCveId`
4. **`src/frontend/src/components/ApprovalDetailModal.tsx`** — same rename
5. **`src/frontend/src/components/ExceptionApprovalDashboard.tsx`** — same rename
6. **`src/frontend/src/components/ExceptionRequestModal.tsx`** — if it reads back the field

**Option B: Fix the backend to match the frontend**

Add a `@SerdeProperty` or rename the backend DTO field to `vulnerabilityCveId`. This would require a backend redeploy and could break MCP tools that reference the current field name.

**Recommendation**: Option A — rename on the frontend side. It's fewer moving parts, no backend redeploy, and no risk of breaking the MCP tools that already use `vulnerabilityCve`.

**Why this mismatch happens**: In Micronaut/Kotlin, the serialized JSON field name directly mirrors the Kotlin property name (`vulnerabilityCve`). JavaScript's permissive object access means `response.vulnerabilityCveId` simply returns `undefined` rather than throwing an error, making this class of bug easy to miss during development and hard to catch without end-to-end testing.

**Prevention tip**: Consider generating TypeScript interfaces from OpenAPI specs or using a shared schema to keep backend/frontend field names in sync automatically.

    17→/**
    18→ * Core business logic service for vulnerability exception request workflow.
    19→ *
    20→ * Responsibilities:
    21→ * - Create exception requests (with auto-approval for ADMIN/SECCHAMPION)
    22→ * - Approve/reject pending requests (with optimistic locking)
    23→ * - Cancel pending requests
    24→ * - Retrieve requests with pagination and filtering
    25→ * - Calculate summary statistics
    26→ * - Create VulnerabilityException entries on approval
    27→ *
    28→ * Concurrency Control:
    29→ * - Uses optimistic locking (@Version field) to prevent race conditions
    30→ * - First-approver-wins: Multiple simultaneous approvals handled gracefully
    31→ * - Throws ConcurrentApprovalException when optimistic lock fails
    32→ *
    33→ * Feature: 031-vuln-exception-approval
    34→ * Reference: quickstart.md lines 35-91, research.md lines 77-110
    35→ */
    36→@Singleton
    37→open class VulnerabilityExceptionRequestService(
    38→    @Inject private val requestRepository: VulnerabilityExceptionRequestRepository,
    39→    @Inject private val vulnerabilityRepository: VulnerabilityRepository,
    40→    @Inject private val vulnerabilityExceptionRepository: VulnerabilityExceptionRepository,
    41→    @Inject private val userRepository: UserRepository,
    42→    @Inject private val auditService: ExceptionRequestAuditService,
    43→    @Inject private val eventPublisher: ApplicationEventPublisher<ExceptionCountChangedEvent>,
    44→    @Inject private val notificationService: ExceptionRequestNotificationService
    45→) {
    46→    private val logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(VulnerabilityExceptionRequestService::class.java)
    47→
    48→    /**
    49→     * Create a new exception request.
    50→     *
    51→     * Auto-approval logic:
    52→     * - If requester has ADMIN or SECCHAMPION role → Status = APPROVED, auto-approval flag = true
    53→     * - Otherwise → Status = PENDING (requires manual approval)
    54→     *
    55→     * @param dto Request creation data
    56→     * @param requesterUserId ID of the user creating the request
    57→     * @param clientIp Client IP address for audit logging (optional)
    58→     * @return Created request DTO
    59→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if vulnerability not found or duplicate active request exists
    60→     */
    61→    @Transactional
    62→    open fun createRequest(
    63→        dto: CreateExceptionRequestDto,
    64→        requesterUserId: Long,
    65→        clientIp: String? = null
    66→    ): VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto {
    67→        logger.debug("Creating exception request: vulnerabilityId={}, userId={}", dto.vulnerabilityId, requesterUserId)
    68→
    69→        // Validate vulnerability exists
    70→        val vulnerability = vulnerabilityRepository.findById(dto.vulnerabilityId).orElseThrow {
    71→            IllegalArgumentException("Vulnerability with ID ${dto.vulnerabilityId} not found")
    72→        }
    73→
    74→        // Get requester user
    75→        val requester = userRepository.findById(requesterUserId).orElseThrow {
    76→            IllegalArgumentException("User with ID $requesterUserId not found")
    77→        }
    78→
    79→        // Check for duplicate active requests (FR-033)
    80→        checkForDuplicateActiveRequest(dto.vulnerabilityId)
    81→
    82→        // Determine if auto-approval applies
    83→        val isAutoApproved = requester.hasRole(User.Role.ADMIN) || requester.hasRole(User.Role.SECCHAMPION)
    84→        val initialStatus = if (isAutoApproved) ExceptionRequestStatus.APPROVED else ExceptionRequestStatus.PENDING
    85→
    86→        // Create request entity (with sanitized reason to prevent XSS)
    87→        val request = VulnerabilityExceptionRequest(
    88→            vulnerability = vulnerability,
    89→            requestedByUser = requester,
    90→            requestedByUsername = requester.username,
    91→            scope = dto.scope,
    92→            reason = sanitizeInput(dto.reason),
    93→            expirationDate = dto.expirationDate,
    94→            status = initialStatus,
    95→            autoApproved = isAutoApproved
    96→        )
    97→
    98→        // If auto-approved, set review fields immediately
    99→        if (isAutoApproved) {
   100→            request.reviewedByUser = requester
   101→            request.reviewedByUsername = requester.username
   102→            request.reviewDate = LocalDateTime.now()
   103→        }
   104→
   105→        // Save request
   106→        val savedRequest = requestRepository.save(request)
   107→
   108→        // Log creation
   109→        auditService.logRequestCreated(savedRequest, requester, clientIp)
   110→
   111→        // If auto-approved, create exception immediately and log approval
   112→        if (isAutoApproved) {
   113→            createExceptionFromRequest(savedRequest)
   114→            auditService.logApproval(savedRequest, requester, clientIp)
   115→            logger.info("Auto-approved exception request: requestId={}, userId={}", savedRequest.id, requesterUserId)
   116→        } else {
   117→            logger.info("Created pending exception request: requestId={}, userId={}", savedRequest.id, requesterUserId)
   118→            // Publish count change event for real-time badge update (pending count increased)
   119→            publishCountChangeEvent()
   120→
   121→            // Send email notifications to ADMIN/SECCHAMPION users (Phase 10)
   122→            try {
   123→                notificationService.notifyAdminsOfNewRequest(savedRequest)
   124→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   125→                logger.error("Failed to send notification for new request {}: {}", savedRequest.id, e.message)
   126→                // Do not throw - email failures should not block request creation
   127→            }
   128→        }
   129→
   130→        return toDto(savedRequest)
   131→    }
   132→
   133→    /**
   134→     * Approve a pending exception request.
   135→     *
   136→     * Creates corresponding VulnerabilityException on successful approval.
   137→     * Uses optimistic locking to prevent concurrent approvals.
   138→     *
   139→     * @param requestId ID of the request to approve
   140→     * @param reviewerUserId ID of the reviewer (ADMIN or SECCHAMPION)
   141→     * @param reviewDto Optional review comment
   142→     * @param clientIp Client IP address for audit logging (optional)
   143→     * @return Updated request DTO
   144→     * @throws ConcurrentApprovalException if request already reviewed by another user
   145→     * @throws IllegalStateException if request not in PENDING status
   146→     */
   147→    @Transactional
   148→    open fun approveRequest(
   149→        requestId: Long,
   150→        reviewerUserId: Long,
   151→        reviewDto: ReviewExceptionRequestDto?,
   152→        clientIp: String? = null
   153→    ): VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto {
   154→        logger.debug("Approving exception request: requestId={}, reviewerId={}", requestId, reviewerUserId)
   155→
   156→        val request = requestRepository.findById(requestId).orElseThrow {
   157→            IllegalArgumentException("Exception request with ID $requestId not found")
   158→        }
   159→
   160→        val reviewer = userRepository.findById(reviewerUserId).orElseThrow {
   161→            IllegalArgumentException("User with ID $reviewerUserId not found")
   162→        }
   163→
   164→        // Validate reviewer has ADMIN or SECCHAMPION role (defense-in-depth)
   165→        if (!reviewer.hasRole(User.Role.ADMIN) && !reviewer.hasRole(User.Role.SECCHAMPION)) {
   166→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Only users with ADMIN or SECCHAMPION role can approve exception requests")
   167→        }
   168→
   169→        // Validate status transition
   170→        if (!request.canTransitionTo(ExceptionRequestStatus.APPROVED)) {
   171→            throw IllegalStateException(
   172→                "Cannot approve request in ${request.status} status. Only PENDING requests can be approved."
   173→            )
   174→        }
   175→
   176→        try {
   177→            // Update request status (optimistic lock check happens here)
   178→            request.status = ExceptionRequestStatus.APPROVED
   179→            request.reviewedByUser = reviewer
   180→            request.reviewedByUsername = reviewer.username
   181→            request.reviewDate = LocalDateTime.now()
   182→            request.reviewComment = reviewDto?.reviewComment?.let { sanitizeInput(it) }
   183→
   184→            val updatedRequest = requestRepository.update(request)
   185→
   186→            // Create corresponding VulnerabilityException
   187→            createExceptionFromRequest(updatedRequest)
   188→
   189→            // Log approval
   190→            auditService.logApproval(updatedRequest, reviewer, clientIp)
   191→
   192→            logger.info("Approved exception request: requestId={}, reviewerId={}", requestId, reviewerUserId)
   193→
   194→            // Publish count change event for real-time badge update (pending count decreased)
   195→            publishCountChangeEvent()
   196→
   197→            // Send email notification to requester (Phase 10)
   198→            try {
   199→                notificationService.notifyRequesterOfApproval(updatedRequest)
   200→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   201→                logger.error("Failed to send approval notification for request {}: {}", requestId, e.message)
   202→                // Do not throw - email failures should not block approval workflow
   203→            }
   204→
   205→            return toDto(updatedRequest)
   206→
   207→        } catch (e: OptimisticLockException) {
   208→            // Concurrent modification detected - another user already reviewed this request
   209→            handleOptimisticLockFailure(requestId, "approve")
   210→        }
   211→    }
   212→
   213→    /**
   214→     * Reject a pending exception request.
   215→     *
   216→     * Does NOT create VulnerabilityException.
   217→     * Uses optimistic locking to prevent concurrent reviews.
   218→     *
   219→     * @param requestId ID of the request to reject
   220→     * @param reviewerUserId ID of the reviewer (ADMIN or SECCHAMPION)
   221→     * @param reviewDto Review comment (REQUIRED for rejection, minimum 10 characters)
   222→     * @param clientIp Client IP address for audit logging (optional)
   223→     * @return Updated request DTO
   224→     * @throws ConcurrentApprovalException if request already reviewed by another user
   225→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if review comment missing or too short
   226→     * @throws IllegalStateException if request not in PENDING status
   227→     */
   228→    @Transactional
   229→    open fun rejectRequest(
   230→        requestId: Long,
   231→        reviewerUserId: Long,
   232→        reviewDto: ReviewExceptionRequestDto,
   233→        clientIp: String? = null
   234→    ): VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto {
   235→        logger.debug("Rejecting exception request: requestId={}, reviewerId={}", requestId, reviewerUserId)
   236→
   237→        // Validate review comment (required for rejection, minimum 10 characters)
   238→        if (reviewDto.reviewComment.isNullOrBlank()) {
   239→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Review comment is required for rejection")
   240→        }
   241→        if (reviewDto.reviewComment.length < 10) {
   242→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Review comment must be at least 10 characters")
   243→        }
   244→
   245→        val request = requestRepository.findById(requestId).orElseThrow {
   246→            IllegalArgumentException("Exception request with ID $requestId not found")
   247→        }
   248→
   249→        val reviewer = userRepository.findById(reviewerUserId).orElseThrow {
   250→            IllegalArgumentException("User with ID $reviewerUserId not found")
   251→        }
   252→
   253→        // Validate reviewer has ADMIN or SECCHAMPION role (defense-in-depth)
   254→        if (!reviewer.hasRole(User.Role.ADMIN) && !reviewer.hasRole(User.Role.SECCHAMPION)) {
   255→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Only users with ADMIN or SECCHAMPION role can reject exception requests")
   256→        }
   257→
   258→        // Validate status transition
   259→        if (!request.canTransitionTo(ExceptionRequestStatus.REJECTED)) {
   260→            throw IllegalStateException(
   261→                "Cannot reject request in ${request.status} status. Only PENDING requests can be rejected."
   262→            )
   263→        }
   264→
   265→        try {
   266→            // Update request status (optimistic lock check happens here)
   267→            request.status = ExceptionRequestStatus.REJECTED
   268→            request.reviewedByUser = reviewer
   269→            request.reviewedByUsername = reviewer.username
   270→            request.reviewDate = LocalDateTime.now()
   271→            request.reviewComment = sanitizeInput(reviewDto.reviewComment)
   272→
   273→            val updatedRequest = requestRepository.update(request)
   274→
   275→            // Log rejection
   276→            auditService.logRejection(updatedRequest, reviewer, clientIp)
   277→
   278→            logger.info("Rejected exception request: requestId={}, reviewerId={}, comment={}",
   279→                requestId, reviewerUserId, reviewDto.reviewComment?.take(50))
   280→
   281→            // Publish count change event for real-time badge update (pending count decreased)
   282→            publishCountChangeEvent()
   283→
   284→            // Send email notification to requester (Phase 10)
   285→            try {
   286→                notificationService.notifyRequesterOfRejection(updatedRequest)
   287→            } catch (e: Exception) {
   288→                logger.error("Failed to send rejection notification for request {}: {}", requestId, e.message)
   289→                // Do not throw - email failures should not block rejection workflow
   290→            }
   291→
   292→            return toDto(updatedRequest)
   293→
   294→        } catch (e: OptimisticLockException) {
   295→            // Concurrent modification detected
   296→            handleOptimisticLockFailure(requestId, "reject")
   297→        }
   298→    }

   300→    /**
   301→     * Cancel a pending exception request (requester only).
   302→     *
   303→     * @param requestId ID of the request to cancel
   304→     * @param requesterUserId ID of the user cancelling (must be original requester)
   305→     * @param clientIp Client IP address for audit logging (optional)
   306→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if user is not the requester
   307→     * @throws IllegalStateException if request not in PENDING status
   308→     */
   309→    @Transactional
   310→    open fun cancelRequest(
   311→        requestId: Long,
   312→        requesterUserId: Long,
   313→        clientIp: String? = null
   314→    ): VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto {
   315→        logger.debug("Cancelling exception request: requestId={}, userId={}", requestId, requesterUserId)
   316→
   317→        val request = requestRepository.findById(requestId).orElseThrow {
   318→            IllegalArgumentException("Exception request with ID $requestId not found")
   319→        }
   320→
   321→        val user = userRepository.findById(requesterUserId).orElseThrow {
   322→            IllegalArgumentException("User with ID $requesterUserId not found")
   323→        }
   324→
   325→        // Validate ownership
   326→        if (request.requestedByUser?.id != requesterUserId) {
   327→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Only the original requester can cancel this request")
   328→        }
   329→
   330→        // Validate status and auto-approval for APPROVED requests
   331→        if (!request.canTransitionTo(ExceptionRequestStatus.CANCELLED)) {
   332→            throw IllegalStateException(
   333→                "Cannot cancel request in ${request.status} status. Only PENDING requests can be cancelled."
   334→            )
   335→        }
   336→
   337→        // Additional validation for APPROVED requests
   338→        if (request.status == ExceptionRequestStatus.APPROVED && !request.autoApproved) {
   339→            throw IllegalStateException(
   340→                "Cannot cancel manually approved requests. Only auto-approved requests can be revoked by the requester."
   341→            )
   342→        }
   343→
   344→        // If cancelling an auto-approved request, delete the associated VulnerabilityException
   345→        if (request.status == ExceptionRequestStatus.APPROVED && request.autoApproved) {
   346→            deleteExceptionForRequest(request)
   347→            logger.info("Deleted VulnerabilityException for auto-approved request: requestId={}", requestId)
   348→        }
   349→
   350→        // Update status
   351→        request.status = ExceptionRequestStatus.CANCELLED
   352→        val updatedRequest = requestRepository.update(request)
   353→
   354→        // Log cancellation
   355→        auditService.logCancellation(updatedRequest, user, clientIp)
   356→
   357→        logger.info("Cancelled exception request: requestId={}, userId={}, autoApproved={}",
   358→            requestId, requesterUserId, request.autoApproved)
   359→
   360→        // Publish count change event for real-time badge update (only if was PENDING)
   361→        publishCountChangeEvent()
   362→
   363→        return toDto(updatedRequest)
   364→    }

   366→    /**
   367→     * Get exception request by ID.
   368→     *
   369→     * @param requestId ID of the request
   370→     * @return Request DTO

## Bug Analysis Report

I've thoroughly examined the data flow for the Exception Approvals page and identified the critical bug causing the count/table mismatch.

### Root Cause

The bug is a **race condition and state management issue** in the frontend component. Here's the problem:

**In `ExceptionApprovalDashboard.tsx` (lines 62-64):**
```typescript
useEffect(() => {
    fetchPendingRequests();
}, [currentPage, pageSize]);
```

The component **only fetches pending requests** when the page or page size changes. It does **NOT** use `getPendingCount()` independently. Instead, it derives the count from the paginated response:

**Line 204:**
```typescript
const pendingCount = pendingRequests?.totalElements || 0;
```

### The Sync Problem

1. **After approval (lines 102-119):**
   - User clicks "Quick Approve"
   - `approveRequest()` is called
   - On success, **only `fetchPendingRequests()` is called** (line 119)
   - This fetches the pending list from `GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/pending?page=0&size=20`

2. **The race condition:**
   - The backend's `approveRequest()` (Service.kt lines 148-211) calls `publishCountChangeEvent()` on line 195 AFTER the database is updated
   - But the frontend's `fetchPendingRequests()` might execute BEFORE the backend has fully committed and published the event
   - More critically: **the frontend never fetches the count endpoint separately**

3. **The display issue:**
   - The count card (lines 286-302) shows `pendingCount` derived from `pendingRequests.totalElements`
   - The table (lines 333-486) shows `pendingRequests.content`
   - If the pagination query returns stale data OR the refresh is partial, they'll show different data

### Why The Bug Manifests

Looking at the frontend service (`exceptionRequestService.ts`):
- **Line 273-303**: `getPendingRequests()` calls `GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/pending?page={}&size={}`
- **Line 311-334**: `getPendingCount()` calls `GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/pending/count`

These are **two separate endpoints** with potentially different data:
1. `pending/count` returns total pending count across ALL pages
2. `pending?page=0&size=20` returns only the first 20 pending items

**But the component never calls `getPendingCount()`** — it only calls `getPendingRequests()` and extracts `totalElements` from that.

### The Data Flow Issue

**Current (broken) flow in ExceptionApprovalDashboard:**
```
Initial load:
  → fetchPendingRequests() 
  → Sets pendingRequests with {totalElements: 5, content: [req1, req2, ...]}
  → Displays count=5, table shows 5 rows ✓

After approval:
  → approveRequest() succeeds
  → fetchPendingRequests() called with currentPage=0, pageSize=20
  → But if timing is off or DB transaction isn't complete:
    → Returns {totalElements: 5, content: [req2, req3, ...]} (old total, updated list)
    → OR returns {totalElements: 4, content: [req1, req2, ...]} (inconsistent state)
  → Displays count=X, table shows Y rows ✗
```

### The Real Root Cause

The **`fetchPendingRequests()` function only has one effect trigger** (lines 62-64):
```typescript
useEffect(() => {
    fetchPendingRequests();
}, [currentPage, pageSize]);  // ← Only triggered by pagination changes
```

After approval, it calls `fetchPendingRequests()` manually (line 119), but this is **not in a useEffect with proper refresh logic**. The component relies on a single fetch being sufficient, but:

1. No debouncing/retry logic if data is stale
2. No separate count fetch to verify the data
3. No mechanism to wait for the backend event to propagate before refetching
4. Pagination state (`currentPage`, `pageSize`) hasn't changed, so a simple re-fetch of the same page might hit a cache or return slightly stale data from the database

### Additional Issue: Missing Batch Refresh

Looking at the modal handlers (lines 133-143):
```typescript
const handleModalApprove = () => {
    // Refresh data after modal approval
    fetchPendingRequests();
    handleCloseDetailModal();
};
```

This also **only refreshes the pending list**, never the count separately.

### Recommended Fixes

1. **Add a separate count fetch** that runs independently to verify the badge count
2. **Implement retry logic** after approval to wait for eventual consistency
3. **Use polling or SSE** for real-time updates (the backend already publishes `ExceptionCountChangedEvent`)
4. **Reset pagination to page 0** after approval to ensure the newest data
5. **Add a small delay** (100-200ms) before refetching to let the database transaction complete

---

### File Locations

**Frontend:**
- `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/frontend/src/components/ExceptionApprovalDashboard.tsx` (lines 62-64, 102-119, 133-143, 204)
- `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/frontend/src/services/exceptionRequestService.ts` (lines 273-303, 311-334)

**Backend:**
- `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/controller/VulnerabilityExceptionRequestController.kt` (lines 443-500)
- `/Users/flake/sources/misc/secman/src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/service/VulnerabilityExceptionRequestService.kt` (lines 431-443)

The backend implementation is correct — both the `/pending` and `/pending/count` endpoints query the same repository using the same `PENDING` status filter. The bug is entirely in the frontend's state management and refresh logic.

    26→/**
    27→ * REST API controller for vulnerability exception request workflow.
    28→ *
    29→ * Endpoints:
    30→ * - POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests - Create new request
    31→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/my - Get user's own requests
    32→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/my/summary - Get user's request statistics
    33→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id} - Get request by ID
    34→ * - POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}/approve - Approve request (ADMIN/SECCHAMPION only)
    35→ * - POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}/reject - Reject request (ADMIN/SECCHAMPION only)
    36→ * - DELETE /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id} - Cancel own pending request
    37→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/pending - Get all pending requests (ADMIN/SECCHAMPION only)
    38→ * - GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/pending/count - Get pending count for badge
    39→ *
    40→ * Concurrency Control: First-approver-wins using optimistic locking
    41→ * Audit Logging: All state transitions logged asynchronously
    42→ *
    43→ * Feature: 031-vuln-exception-approval
    44→ * Reference: contracts/exception-request-api.yaml, quickstart.md
    45→ */
    46→@Controller("/api/vulnerability-exception-requests")

    48→open class VulnerabilityExceptionRequestController(
    49→    private val exceptionRequestService: VulnerabilityExceptionRequestService,
    50→    private val statisticsService: ExceptionRequestStatisticsService,
    51→    private val exportService: ExceptionRequestExportService
    52→) {
    53→    private val logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(VulnerabilityExceptionRequestController::class.java)

    65→    /**
    66→     * TASK-016: Create a new exception request.
    67→     *
    68→     * POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests
    69→     * Auth: All authenticated users
    70→     * Auto-approval: If user has ADMIN or SECCHAMPION role, status = APPROVED immediately
    71→     *
    72→     * @param dto Request creation data (vulnerabilityId, scope, reason, expirationDate)
    73→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user
    74→     * @param request HTTP request for IP address extraction
    75→     * @return 201 Created with request DTO, 400 if validation fails, 409 if duplicate active request
    76→     */
    77→    @Post
    78→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
    79→    open fun createRequest(
    80→        @Valid @Body dto: CreateExceptionRequestDto,
    81→        authentication: Authentication,
    82→        request: HttpRequest<*>
    83→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
    84→        return try {
    85→            val userId = getUserId(authentication)
    86→            val clientIp = request.remoteAddress.address.hostAddress
    87→
    88→            logger.debug(
    89→                "Creating exception request: userId={}, vulnerabilityId={}, scope={}",
    90→                userId, dto.vulnerabilityId, dto.scope
    91→            )
    92→
    93→            val createdRequest = exceptionRequestService.createRequest(dto, userId, clientIp)
    94→
    95→            logger.info(
    96→                "Exception request created: requestId={}, status={}, autoApproved={}",
    97→                createdRequest.id, createdRequest.status, createdRequest.autoApproved
    98→            )
    99→
   100→            HttpResponse.created(createdRequest)
   101→
   102→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   103→            logger.warn("Invalid request: {}", e.message)
   104→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(
   105→                error = e.message ?: "Invalid request",
   106→                status = 400
   107→            ))
   108→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   109→            logger.error("Failed to create exception request", e)
   110→            HttpResponse.serverError<ErrorResponse>()
   111→        }
   112→    }

   193→     *
   194→     * @param id Request ID
   195→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user
   196→     * @return 200 OK with request DTO, 403 if not owner (unless ADMIN/SECCHAMPION), 404 if not found

   198→    @Get("/{id}")
   199→    @Secured(SecurityRule.IS_AUTHENTICATED)
   200→    open fun getRequestById(
   201→        @PathVariable id: Long,
   202→        authentication: Authentication
   203→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   204→        return try {
   205→            val userId = getUserId(authentication)
   206→            val request = exceptionRequestService.getRequestById(id)
   207→
   208→            // Access control: User must own request OR have ADMIN/SECCHAMPION role
   209→            val isOwner = request.requestedByUsername == authentication.name
   210→            val isAdminOrSecChampion = hasRole(authentication, "ADMIN") || hasRole(authentication, "SECCHAMPION")
   211→
   212→            if (!isOwner && !isAdminOrSecChampion) {
   213→                logger.warn("Access denied: userId={} attempted to view request {}", userId, id)
   214→                return HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN).body(
   215→                    ErrorResponse(
   216→                        error = "Access denied",
   217→                        details = "You can only view your own requests",
   218→                        status = 403
   219→                    )
   220→                )
   221→            }
   222→
   223→            HttpResponse.ok(request)
   224→
   225→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   226→            logger.warn("Request not found: id={}", id)
   227→            HttpResponse.notFound<VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto>()
   228→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   229→            logger.error("Failed to get request by ID: id={}", id, e)
   230→            HttpResponse.serverError<VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto>()

   234→    /**
   235→     * TASK-020: Approve a pending exception request.
   236→     *
   237→     * POST /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/{id}/approve
   238→     * Auth: ADMIN or SECCHAMPION only
   239→     * Concurrency: First-approver-wins (optimistic locking)
   240→     *
   241→     * @param id Request ID
   242→     * @param reviewDto Optional review comment
   243→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user (reviewer)
   244→     * @param request HTTP request for IP address extraction
   245→     * @return 200 OK with updated request, 404 if not found, 409 if concurrent approval
   246→     */
   247→    @Post("/{id}/approve")
   248→    @Secured("ADMIN", "SECCHAMPION")
   249→    open fun approveRequest(
   250→        @PathVariable id: Long,
   251→        @Nullable @Body reviewDto: ReviewExceptionRequestDto?,
   252→        authentication: Authentication,
   253→        request: HttpRequest<*>
   254→    ): HttpResponse<*> {
   255→        return try {
   256→            val reviewerId = getUserId(authentication)
   257→            val clientIp = request.remoteAddress.address.hostAddress
   258→
   259→            logger.debug("Approving request: requestId={}, reviewerId={}", id, reviewerId)
   260→
   261→            val approvedRequest = exceptionRequestService.approveRequest(
   262→                requestId = id,
   263→                reviewerUserId = reviewerId,
   264→                reviewDto = reviewDto,
   265→                clientIp = clientIp
   266→            )
   267→
   268→            logger.info("Request approved: requestId={}, reviewerId={}", id, reviewerId)
   269→
   270→            HttpResponse.ok(approvedRequest)
   271→
   272→        } catch (e: ConcurrentApprovalException) {
   273→            // TASK-025: Handle concurrent approval conflicts
   274→            logger.warn("Concurrent approval conflict: requestId={}, {}", id, e.getDetailedMessage())
   275→            HttpResponse.status<ErrorResponse>(HttpStatus.CONFLICT).body(
   276→                ErrorResponse(
   277→                    error = e.getUserMessage(),
   278→                    details = e.getDetailedMessage(),
   279→                    status = 409
   280→                )
   281→            )
   282→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   283→            logger.warn("Invalid approval request: requestId={}, error={}", id, e.message)
   284→            HttpResponse.notFound<VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto>()
   285→        } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
   286→            // TASK-026: Handle invalid status transitions
   287→            logger.warn("Invalid status transition: requestId={}, error={}", id, e.message)
   288→            HttpResponse.badRequest(ErrorResponse(
   289→                error = "Invalid status transition",
   290→                details = e.message,
   291→                status = 400
   292→            ))
   293→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   294→            logger.error("Failed to approve request: requestId={}", id, e)
   295→            HttpResponse.serverError<VulnerabilityExceptionRequestDto>()
   296→        }
   297→    }

   502→    /**
   503→     * GET /api/vulnerability-exception-requests/statistics
   504→     *
   505→     * Get statistical metrics for exception requests.
   506→     *
   507→     * **Access Control**: ADMIN and SECCHAMPION roles only
   508→     *
   509→     * **Query Parameters**:
   510→     * - dateRange: Optional (7days, 30days, 90days, alltime), default 30days
   511→     *
   512→     * **Response**: 200 OK with ExceptionStatisticsDto containing:
   513→     * - totalRequests: Total count in date range
   514→     * - approvalRatePercent: Approval percentage (null if no reviewed requests)
   515→     * - averageApprovalTimeHours: Median approval time in hours (null if no approved requests)
   516→     * - requestsByStatus: Map of status → count
   517→     * - topRequesters: Top 10 requesters with counts
   518→     * - topCVEs: Top 10 CVEs with counts
   519→     *
   520→     * Feature: 031-vuln-exception-approval
   521→     * User Story 8: Analytics & Reporting (P3)
   522→     * Phase 11: Analytics & Reporting
   523→     * Reference: spec.md acceptance scenario US8-1
   524→     */
   525→    @Get("/statistics")
   526→    @Secured("ADMIN", "SECCHAMPION")
   527→    open fun getStatistics(
   528→        authentication: Authentication,
   529→        @QueryValue @Nullable dateRange: String?
   530→    ): HttpResponse<ExceptionStatisticsDto> {
   531→        return try {
   532→            val effectiveDateRange = dateRange ?: "30days"
   533→
   534→            logger.info("Statistics requested: dateRange={}, user={}", effectiveDateRange, authentication.name)
   535→
   536→            // Get metrics from statistics service
   537→            val approvalRate = statisticsService.getApprovalRate(effectiveDateRange)
   538→            val averageApprovalTime = statisticsService.getAverageApprovalTime(effectiveDateRange)
   539→            val requestsByStatus = statisticsService.getRequestsByStatus(effectiveDateRange)
   540→            val topRequesters = statisticsService.getTopRequesters(10, effectiveDateRange)
   541→            val topCVEs = statisticsService.getTopCVEs(10, effectiveDateRange)
   542→
   543→            // Calculate total requests
   544→            val totalRequests = requestsByStatus.values.sum()
   545→
   546→            // Convert status map to string keys for JSON serialization
   547→            val statusMap = requestsByStatus.mapKeys { it.key.name }
   548→
   549→            // Convert to DTOs
   550→            val topRequesterDtos = topRequesters.map { (username, count) ->
   551→                TopRequesterDto(username, count)
   552→            }
   553→            val topCVEDtos = topCVEs.map { (cveId, count) ->
   554→                TopCVEDto(cveId, count)
   555→            }
   556→
   557→            val statistics = ExceptionStatisticsDto(
   558→                totalRequests = totalRequests,
   559→                approvalRatePercent = approvalRate,
   560→                averageApprovalTimeHours = averageApprovalTime,
   561→                requestsByStatus = statusMap,
   562→                topRequesters = topRequesterDtos,
   563→                topCVEs = topCVEDtos
   564→            )
   565→
   566→            logger.debug("Statistics computed: totalRequests={}, approvalRate={}, avgTime={}h",
   567→                totalRequests, approvalRate, averageApprovalTime)
   568→
   569→            HttpResponse.ok(statistics)
   570→
   571→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   572→            logger.error("Failed to get statistics: dateRange={}", dateRange, e)
   573→            HttpResponse.serverError<ExceptionStatisticsDto>()
   574→        }
   575→    }

   580→     * Export exception requests to Excel with optional filtering.
   581→     *
   582→     * **Access Control**: ADMIN and SECCHAMPION roles only
   583→     *
   584→     * **Query Parameters**:
   585→     * - status: Optional status filter (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, EXPIRED, CANCELLED)
   586→     * - dateRange: Optional (7days, 30days, 90days, alltime)
   587→     * - requesterId: Optional requester user ID filter
   588→     * - reviewerId: Optional reviewer user ID filter
   589→     *
   590→     * **Response**: 200 OK with Excel file
   591→     * - Content-Type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet
   592→     * - Filename: exception-requests-{timestamp}.xlsx
   593→     * - Columns: Request ID, CVE ID, Asset Name, Asset IP, Requester, Submission Date,
   594→     *   Status, Reviewer, Review Date, Reason, Review Comment, Expiration Date, Auto-Approved
   595→     *
   596→     * Feature: 031-vuln-exception-approval
   597→     * User Story 8: Analytics & Reporting (P3)
   598→     * Phase 11: Analytics & Reporting
   599→     * Reference: spec.md FR-026, acceptance scenario US8-3
   600→     */
   601→    @Get("/export")
   602→    @Secured("ADMIN", "SECCHAMPION")
   603→    @Produces("application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet")
   604→    open fun exportToExcel(
   605→        authentication: Authentication,
   606→        @QueryValue @Nullable status: String?,
   607→        @QueryValue @Nullable dateRange: String?,
   608→        @QueryValue @Nullable requesterId: Long?,
   609→        @QueryValue @Nullable reviewerId: Long?
   610→    ): HttpResponse<ByteArray> {
   611→        return try {
   612→            logger.info("Excel export requested: status={}, dateRange={}, requesterId={}, reviewerId={}, user={}",
   613→                status, dateRange, requesterId, reviewerId, authentication.name)
   614→
   615→            // Parse status if provided
   616→            val statusEnum = status?.let {
   617→                try {
   618→                    ExceptionRequestStatus.valueOf(it.uppercase())
   619→                } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
   620→                    logger.warn("Invalid status parameter: {}", it)
   621→                    null
   622→                }
   623→            }
   624→
   625→            // Generate Excel export
   626→            val excelData = exportService.exportToExcel(
   627→                status = statusEnum,
   628→                dateRange = dateRange,
   629→                requesterId = requesterId,
   630→                reviewerId = reviewerId
   631→            )
   632→
   633→            // Generate filename with timestamp
   634→            val timestamp = java.time.LocalDateTime.now().format(
   635→                java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("yyyyMMdd-HHmmss")
   636→            )
   637→            val filename = "exception-requests-$timestamp.xlsx"
   638→
   639→            logger.info("Excel export completed: filename={}, size={} bytes", filename, excelData.size())
   640→
   641→            HttpResponse.ok(excelData.toByteArray())
   642→                .header("Content-Disposition", "attachment; filename=\"$filename\"")
   643→
   644→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   645→            logger.error("Failed to export Excel: status={}, dateRange={}", status, dateRange, e)
   646→            HttpResponse.serverError<ByteArray>()
   647→        }
   648→    }
   649→
   650→    /**
   651→     * Extract user ID from authentication object.
   652→     *
   653→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user
   654→     * @return User ID as Long
   655→     * @throws IllegalStateException if user ID not found in attributes
   656→     */
   657→    private fun getUserId(authentication: Authentication): Long {
   658→        return authentication.attributes["userId"]?.toString()?.toLong()
   659→            ?: throw IllegalStateException("User ID not found in authentication context")
   660→    }
   661→
   662→    /**
   663→     * Check if authentication has a specific role.
   664→     *
   665→     * @param authentication Current authenticated user
   666→     * @param role Role name to check
   667→     * @return True if user has the role
   668→     */
   669→    private fun hasRole(authentication: Authentication, role: String): Boolean {
   670→        return authentication.roles.contains(role)
   671→    }
   672→}
